issue.title: Bug: Ctrl+W and Ctrl+T in terminal close/open Tiki tabs instead of going to the shell

phases.title: Gate Ctrl+W and remap Ctrl+T → Ctrl+Shift+T in TerminalPane
verification: TerminalPane.tsx has no Ctrl+T branch, has a new Ctrl+Shift+T branch that calls addTab(), and the Ctrl+W branch contains a `.closest('.xterm')` early-return before `removeTab`.
`cargo check` from apps/desktop/src-tauri runs clean (sanity, no Rust changes expected).
Local TypeScript verification deferred to CI: .github/workflows/pr.yml runs `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm build` (`tsc -b`, stricter than `--noEmit`) on the PR.
Manual smoke when the desktop app can be built: focus a terminal, type `echo hello world`, press Ctrl+W — readline deletes `world`, the tab does NOT close.
Manual smoke: from a focused terminal press Ctrl+T — no new tab opens; the shell receives transpose-chars (or its platform equivalent).
Manual smoke: from a focused terminal press Ctrl+Shift+T — a new terminal tab opens.
Manual smoke: click the tab bar (outside any .xterm container), press Ctrl+W — the active tab closes (regression check that the gate doesn't break the normal close path).
Manual smoke: from a focused terminal press Ctrl+Shift+H — still splits horizontally (regression check that the gate is scoped to Ctrl+W only).
Manual smoke: tooltip on the `+` button in the tab bar reads 'New terminal (Ctrl+Shift+T)'.
